data.title: GQG Partners' Chairman Buys Chess Depositary Interests
data.description:
GQG Partners Chairman and Chief Investment Officer Rajiv Jain acquired 63,402 Chess Depositary Interests (CDIs) at AU$2.15 per CDI by way of an on-market trade, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Jain now holds about 2 million CDIs, per the filing. He also owns 2.07 billion common shares.

Shares of the company rose nearly 1% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on March 28
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira traded at 38.0150 against the U.S. dollar at 0433 GMT, little changed from its close of 38.01 on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.38% lower at 9,612.84 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks fell with heavy selling in South Korea and Japan, while safe-haven gold hit a record high as the latest tariff salvo from U.S. President Donald Trump stoked investor worries of an all-out trade war.

ERDOGAN

President Tayyip Erdogan will attend an inauguration ceremony of a mosque that bears his name via videoconference (1200 GMT) and then a fast breaking dinner in Istanbul (1631 GMT).

JAILED ISTANBUL MAYOR

Turkish authorities detained a lawyer the jailed Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, an MP of the main opposition party, CHP, said on Friday.

MAIN OPPOSITION

Main opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozer will meet the head of the Party of European Socialists Stefan Lofven in Istanbul (14.30 GMT).

ECONOMIC CONFIDENCE (TRECON=ECI)

The Turkish Statistical Institute will announce the March economic confidence index (0700 GMT).

DEBT STOCK

Turkish treasury will announce debt stock for the fourth quarter of 2024 (0900 GMT).

FX-PROTECTED ACCOUNTS

The Central Bank will announce its monthly data on money and banking, including on FX-protected KKM accounts (1130 GMT).

data.title: Guangzhou Zhujiang Brewery's Profit Jumps 30%, Operating Income Rises 7%
data.description:
Guangzhou Zhujiang Brewery's net profit attributable to shareholders in 2024 jumped 30% to 810.3 million yuan from 623.5 million yuan a year earlier, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

Earnings per share climbed 32% year on year to 0.37 yuan from 0.28 yuan.

The Chinese brewery's operating income grew 6.6% to 5.73 billion yuan from 5.38 billion yuan in the prior year, the disclosure said.

The company's shares fell less than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: China, HK stocks drop as investors remain wary of US tariff risks
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks fell broadly on Friday as investor sentiment cooled due to concerns that new U.S. tariffs could shake the global economy.

** At the midday break, the Shanghai Composite index was down 0.71% at 3,349.68 points.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 index was down 0.55%, with its financial sector sub-index lower by 0.39%, the consumer staples sector down 0.83%, the real estate index down 0.47% and the healthcare sub-index up 0.03%.

** Semiconductor (.CSI931865) and energy stocks dropped 2.1% and 1.5%, respectively, to lead the declines.

** The Chinese H-share index listed in Hong Kong, the Hang Seng China Enterprises Index , fell 1.1% to 8,582.09, while the Hang Seng Index (.HIS) was down 0.89% at 23,368.67.

** Hong Kong-listed tech giants lost 1.7%.

** Investors are still gauging the 25% tariff on auto imports announced by U.S. President Donald Trump and its impact on major exporting economies.

** Trump also plans to implement new reciprocal tariffs on several trading partners from April 2.

** Sentiment towards mainland Chinese stocks dropped with lower trading volumes, Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Laura Wang, said in a note on Friday.

** "We expect higher volatility amid tariff uncertainty but believe green shoots on earnings and liquidity warrant further index upside towards year-end," they said.

** The smaller Shenzhen index was down 0.73%, the start-up board ChiNext Composite index was weaker by 0.58% and Shanghai's tech-focused STAR50 index was down 0.75%​.

** Around the region, MSCI's Asia ex-Japan stock index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) was weaker by 0.71% while Japan's Nikkei index was down 2.33%.

data.title: Singapore Manufactured Products, Domestic Supply Price Indices Fall in February
data.description:
Singapore's Manufactured Products Price Index fell 0.5% in February, reversing a 3.6% gain in January, the Department of Statistics reported Friday.

The oil index dropped 2.6%, while the non-oil index slipped 0.2%, weighed down by declines in machinery, transport equipment, and manufactured goods.

Year-on-year, the Manufactured Products Price Index rose 5.5%, following a 6.1% increase in January. The oil index fell by 5.3%, but the non-oil index gained by 7.5%, driven by higher prices for electrical machinery, food, and live animals.

Meanwhile, Singapore's Domestic Supply Price Index fell 0.7% in February after a 3.6% rise in January. The oil index declined 1.8%, while the non-oil index dropped 0.3%, driven by lower machinery, transport equipment, and manufactured goods prices.

Year-on-year, the Domestic Supply Price Index rose 5.1% after a 5.5% increase in January. The oil index fell by 4.5%, but the non-oil index climbed by 8.9%, driven by higher electrical machinery and food prices.

data.title: iFast Appoints Group CFO
data.description:
iFast will appoint Terence Lin Weide as the group chief financial officer, effective April 1, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Wiede will succeed Lim Kian Thong, who will be reaching retirement age.

data.title: Getlink Prices EUR600 Million Senior Green Notes
data.description:
Getlink priced on Thursday its offering of 600 million euros in 4.125% senior secured green notes due 2030 at a price equal to the principal amount.

The French tunnel and railway operator intends to close the issuance on April 4, subject to customary conditions.

Proceeds from the issuance will be used for the company's plan to redeem its existing 850 million euros of senior secured green notes that will mature in October.

data.title: Zibo Xintai Petrochemical mulls turnaround at Group II base oil unit
data.description: Zibo Xintai Petrochemical is in plans to shut down its Group II base oil unit in Shandong.A Polymerupdate source in China informed a team member,

data.title: Guocoland Places Dormant Subsidiary Under Voluntary Liquidation
data.description:
Guocoland placed dormant indirect wholly owned subsidiary, GLL EWI (HK) under member's voluntary liquidation, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The liquidation is not expected to have an impact on the company's earnings per share for the year ending June 30.

data.title: Shmoh Almadi Logs Lower FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Shmoh Almadi (SASE:9622) said Thursday that its net profit and revenue for full-year 2024 dropped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 9.5 million Saudi riyals, compared with 16 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 1.18 riyals from 2 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed food company's revenue was 131.6 million riyals, compared with 143 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: Axiata Group: Appointed Patrick Forth as Independent Nonexecutive Director, Effective Immediately
FX option expiries for 28 March 10am New York cut
data.description:
There are a couple to take note of on the day, as highlighted in bold.

The first ones are for EUR/USD at the 1.0750 and 1.0800 levels once again. That should help to keep price action locked in once again, with the latter especially providing an extra layer of defense to any upside extensions on the day. For the week itself, the 100-week moving average at 1.0780 remains a crucial technical level to be mindful of.

Then, there is one for USD/JPY at the 151.00 level. If anything else, the expiries could provide a magnet for price action to weave in and about the figure level in the session ahead at least. That before we get to US trading later with the PCE price index release in focus as well. But all else being equal, the risk mood and bond yields will continue to be the bigger driver of trading sentiment for the pair today.

For more information on how to use this data, you may refer to this post here.

data.title: African Markets - Factors to watch on March 28
data.description:
The following company announcements, scheduled economic indicators, debt and currency market moves and political events may affect African markets on Friday.

- - - - -

EVENTS:

The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe to announce its first interest rates decision for 2025.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks fell on Friday with heavy selling in South Korea and Japan while safe-haven gold hit a record high as the latest tariff salvo from U.S. President Donald Trump stoked investor worries of an all-out trade war.

WORLD OIL PRICES

Oil prices held near one-month highs on Friday and were poised for a third weekly gain on a tightening global supply outlook after the U.S. imposed tariffs against countries buying oil and gas from Venezuela and placed restrictions on Iranian oil trade.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

AFRICA STOCKS

For the latest news on African stocks, click on

SOUTH AFRICA MARKETS

South Africa's rand weakened on Thursday as markets fretted over U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs due next week.

KENYA MARKETS

The Kenyan shilling was stable against the dollar on Thursday, data from the London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) showed.

AFRICA MARKETS

The Kenyan, Ugandan, Nigerian and Ghanaian currencies are expected to be broadly stable against the dollar in the next week to Thursday, while Zambia's is seen under pressure, traders said.

KENYA IMF

The International Monetary Fund said on Thursday that Kenya's cash shortage requires a new spending strategy as it confirmed the government requested a new program.

SOUTH SUDAN BRITAIN

Britain on Thursday advised its citizens in South Sudan to leave the country following rising tensions that have brought the East African nation to the brink of renewed civil war.

CHAD MIGRATION

The U.S. embassy in Chad has suspended the issuance of most nonimmigrant visas for 90 days, the State Department said on Thursday, in the first known international travel restrictions of President Donald Trump's second term.

CONGO HUNGER

Twenty-eight million people face acute hunger in the Democratic Republic of Congo, a record for the country, driven by an escalating conflict between the government and Rwandan-backed rebels in the east, the United Nations said on Thursday.

SOUTH SUDAN SECURITY

The detention of South Sudan's First Vice President Riek Machar has effectively collapsed the peace deal that ended the 2013-2018 civil war, his party said on Thursday.

data.title: Arab Sea Information Systems' FY24 Net Loss Widens; Revenue Up
data.description:
Arab Sea Information Systems Co. (SASE:7201) said Thursday that its net loss for full-year 2024 widened, while revenue rose year over year.

Net loss for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 17.7 million Saudi riyals, compared with a net loss of 9.8 million riyals earlier. Loss per share moved to 0.18 riyal from a loss per share of 0.09 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed software and IT services company's revenue was 43.3 million riyals, compared with 41.2 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: Tibbiyah Swings to Loss in FY24; Revenue Climbs
data.description:
Arabian International Healthcare Holding Co. (SASE:9530), d/b/a Tibbiyah, said Thursday it swung to a loss in full-year 2024, while revenue rose year over year.

Net loss for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 66.6 million Saudi riyals, compared with a profit of 38.1 million riyals earlier. Loss per share moved to 3.37 riyals from an EPS of 1.92 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed medical systems and equipment provider's revenue was 814.4 million riyals, compared with 806.9 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: Palm Oil Rallies, Set for Modest Weekly Gains
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures jumped near 2.0% to around MYR 4,390 per tonne, marking the third session of gains.

The upturn followed news that U.S. oil and biofuel producers had agreed in principle to raise the mandate for renewable diesel and biodiesel.

Meanwhile, key buyer China increased palm oil purchases amid a trade dispute with Canada.

For the week, the contracts are set for their first rise in three weeks, up around 0.5% so far, supported by weather uncertainties.

Forecasts indicate a transition from La Niña to a neutral El Niño phase between March and May, which could impact output.

Meanwhile, Indonesia raised its crude palm oil reference price for April to USD 961.54 per metric ton while keeping a $124 per ton export tax.

However, gains were capped by concerns over labor shortages after the Eid ul-Fitr break next week.

On the export front, weak shipments are expected, as data from cargo surveyors showed a fall of between 8.1% to 8.5% from February during the March 1-25 period.

data.title: Global Unveiling of Inner Mongolia's 2025 Spring Tourism Event: Inviting the World to Enjoy a Culture and Nature Symphony of the Northern Frontier
data.description:
HOHHOT, China, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Recently, the Culture and Tourism Department of the Inner Mongolia autonomous region has announced a "Musical Tours in Inner Mongolia" series tourism product for 2025, which centered on the "culture of the northern frontier" will offer 219 cultural and tourist activities across six themes, inviting global travelers to immerse themselves in the vibrant spring of the grasslands. From the romantic sea of flowers along the Yellow River to the thrill of desert adventures, and from ancient intangible cultural heritage to modern artistic performances, this cultural and tourist feast from April through June weaves together nature, tradition, and innovation to unfold a beautiful tapestry of China's northern frontier to the world.

A Blossoming Canvas of Nature and Culture

As winter gives way to spring between late March and May, Inner Mongolia bursts into life along the "Great Bend" of the Yellow River, where pear and apricot blossoms paint the landscape in hues of pink and white. Visitors can wander the "Apricot Blossom and Flower Festival" trail in Tumed Right Banner of Baotou or hike through the ancient forests of the Greater Khingan Mountains to marvel at alpine azaleas blooming alongside remaining ice and snow. Meanwhile, featured activities jointly held by leagues and cities like Hohhot's Wusutu Apricot Flowers Festival and the ecological observation of Huazi fish (Leuciscus waleckii) migration in Chifeng transform "flower tourism" into immersive experiences, blending photography contests, open-air concerts, and nature education programs to celebrate the splendor of spring beyond visual enjoyment.

Deeper in the grasslands, a dialogue between heritage and modernity is unfolding quietly. The Ordos Horsehead Fiddle Symphony Orchestra will perform epic compositions against the vast horizon of the grasslands, while 30 nationwide tours of the "Spring Village Evening" bring intangible cultural heritage to life through folk songs and dances staged in scenic settings. From April 25 to 26, Rene Liu will headline two concerts in Hohhot, paired with travel packages offering tickets of scenic areas at a discount, themed hotel packages, and culinary guides, enticing music fans and tourists alike to explore the hidden gems of the region.

Contemporary Expression of Millennium-long Nomadic Culture

Spring in Inner Mongolia also heralds the renewal of nomadic culture. In Alxa's deserts, camel races and Mongolian wrestling competitions stir the spirit of adventure, while activities like sandboarding and stargazing camps allow visitors to connect with the magnificence of nature. On the Hulunbuir prairie, tourists can join shepherds in traditional tasks like wool shearing and milking, or participate in Xilingol's Mongolian Horse Super League, galloping across 200 kilometers of prairie and sand dunes.

Cultural inheritance also attracts great attention through immersive experiences. The live-action drama in Baotou, "Melodies in Baotou - Love for the Yellow River", reenacts ancient folk customs against the backdrop of the thawing Yellow River, while activities such as migratory bird observation on the Wuhai Lake merge ecological awareness with travel. Workshops led by intangible cultural inheritors invite visitors to craft horsehead fiddles and practice Mongolian embroidery, transforming museum artifacts into living traditions that resonate through touch and creativity.

Source: The Culture and Tourism Department of the Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region

Contact person: Mr. Hao, Tel: 86-10-63074558

data.title: Turkey detains lawyer of jailed Istanbul mayor, main opposition party says
data.description:
Turkish authorities detained a lawyer for President Tayyip Erdogan's biggest political rival, jailed Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, an MP of the main opposition party, CHP, said on Friday.

Imamoglu, a CHP member who leads Erdoguan in some polls, was jailed on Sunday pending trial for graft, provoking the largest anti-government protests in a decade, which led in turn to mass arrests nationwide.

Mehmet Pehlivan, a lawyer who defended Imamoglu in the latest investigation, was detained "for fabricated reasons", CHP MP Turan Taskin Ozer said in a post on X, without giving details.

Imamoglu, who has been temporarily dismissed from his job because of the case, demanded the immediate release of his lawyer.

"As if the coup on democracy wasn't enough, they can't stand the victims of this coup defending themselves," Imamoglu said on X.

data.title: Hexagon Purus Books Order for Hydrogen Fuel Storage Systems from Stadler Rail
data.description:
Hexagon Purus said Thursday it received an order for hydrogen fuel storage systems from rail applications manufacturer Stadler Rail .

The multi-year deal concerns the Norwegian mobility and infrastructure company's type 4 hydrogen fuel storage systems that it intends to produce from its site in Kassel, Germany. The systems will be used for hydrogen rail projects in the US state of California.

data.title: Stovec Industries CFO Resigns; Shares Slip 1%
data.description:
Stovec Industries said Paras Mehta has resigned as chief financial officer and key managerial person of the company, according to an Indian bourse filing on Thursday.

Mehta resigned to seek opportunity outside the company, the filing said.

Shares of the company were down 0.8% in early trading.

data.title: Amoytop Biotech 2024 Profit Up 49%, Revenue Rises 34%
data.description:
Xiamen Amoytop Biotech posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 827.6 million yuan, up 49% from 555.4 million yuan a year earlier.

Earnings per share climbed 48% year over year to 2.03 yuan from 1.37 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The biotech company's revenue rose 34% to 2.82 billion yuan from 2.10 billion yuan.

Xiamen Amoytop Biotech declared a 2024 dividend of 0.62 yuan per share.

Shares of the company rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: New Zealand Shares Fall Amid Rising Global Trade War Fears
data.description:
New Zealand shares saw another session of losses on Friday, along with other Asian indexes, as markets continued to digest an uncertain global outlook because of US tariffs.

The S&P/NZX 50 Index fell 0.2% or 18.33 points to close at 12,287.46.

US President Donald Trump announced plans to impose a 25% tariff on all foreign-made vehicles and auto parts, a move that has led companies like Volvo and Hyundai to relocate some of their production to the US.

Meanwhile, continued global business outlook fears sent gold to yet another record high.

On the local front, New Zealand's consumer confidence fell in March, with declines across nearly all components, according to a survey by ANZ Research and Roy Morgan Research released Friday.

Also, the number of enterprise entries in New Zealand rose to 4,167 in February, compared with 3,084 in January, Stats NZ data showed Friday.

New Zealand's seasonally adjusted filled jobs across all industries were flat at nearly 2.4 million in February, following a 0.1% uptick in the prior month, Stats NZ data showed Friday.

In corporate news, Hallenstein Glasson Holdings reported Friday fiscal half-year earnings of NZ$0.355 per diluted share, unchanged from the year-earlier period. The company's shares edged up at market close.

Sky Network Television secured an eight-year exclusive broadcast right to air Six Nations and Gallagher Premiership Rugby in New Zealand. The company's Kiwi shares finished modestly higher.

data.title: Singapore's Import, Export Prices Fall in February
data.description:
Singapore's Import Price Index fell 3.3% year on year in February, extending the 2.2% decrease in the preceding month, the Department of Statistics reported Friday.

The Non-Oil Index was down 3.2%, while the Oil Index dropped 3.7%.

The Export Price Index also slipped 3.3% year on year in February, following a 2.2% decrease in the previous month. The non-oil index slid 2.4%, while the oil index dipped 7.1% on year.

On a month-on-month basis, the Import Price Index fell 1.5% in February, reversing a 0.8% increase a month before, while the Export Price Index was down 0.5% during the month, after a 0.8% increase in the previous month, the report said.

data.title: IT, auto drag India's benchmarks as tariff worries weigh
data.description:
Indian benchmark indexes fell on Friday, dragged by IT and auto stocks as uncertainty over U.S. reciprocal tariffs that are to be announced next week made investors cautious.

The NSE Nifty 50 was down 0.17% to 23,553.8, while the BSE Sensex traded 0.20% lower at 77,451 as of 10:06 a.m. IST.

Other Asian markets fell, dragged by auto stocks, after U.S. President Donald Trump announced a 25% tariff on automotive imports, due to kick in next week.

"The market will remain subdued in the next couple of sessions due to uncertainties surrounding the U.S. tariffs, which will influence the immediate trajectory and keep investors cautious," said Sameet Chavan, head of research at Angel One.

Trump is expected to implement retaliatory tariffs on April 2 but the U.S. administration has indicated that not all tariffs will be levied.

Indian auto shares lost 0.8% on the day, taking their two-day decline to 2%.

Pharma stocks fell 0.9%, also weighed down by tariff worries. The U.S. accounts for about one-third of India's total pharmaceutical exports.

Information technology companies , which earn a major share of their revenue from the U.S., lost 1.3% ahead of a key U.S. inflation reading due after Indian market hours.

Eight of the ten Nifty IT constituents declined. Tata Consultancy Services and Infosys fell 1.1% and 1.7%, respectively.

Bucking the trend, exchange operator BSE surged 12% after the country's markets regulator rejigged the derivatives expiry schedule, a move analysts said will help ease concerns over the bourse's market share loss.

The broader, more domestically focussed smallcaps and midcaps gained 0.6% and 0.4%, respectively.

($1 = 85.6830 Indian rupees)

data.title: New Zealand Shares Lose 0.15%, But Snap Run of Weekly Losses — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 closed 0.15% lower at 12287.46 under the weight of its largest five stocks, but still snapped a run of five consecutive weekly losses. The benchmark index spent Friday's session bouncing between small gains and losses, before finishing in the red amid retreats by Infratil, Meridian Energy, Auckland International Airport, Mercury NZ and Fisher & Paykel. Medical-device maker Fisher & Paykel was the biggest loser of the group, shedding 1.5% amid continued worries over tariffs' impact on global trade. Elsewhere, logistics operators Mainfreight and Freightways gave up 1.6% and 1.9%, respectively. The NZX-50 rose 1.4% for the week. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Li Ning's 2024 Profit Slides About 6%; Shares Rise 3%
data.description:
Li Ning's attributable profit fell 5.5% to 3.01 billion yuan in 2024 from 3.19 billion yuan in 2023, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the sports apparel maker decreased to 1.1652 yuan from 1.2266 yuan a year earlier. Analysts at Visible Alpha estimated EPS at 1.19 yuan.

Revenue grew 3.9% to 28.7 billion yuan from 27.6 billion yuan in the previous year. Visible Alpha analysts forecast revenue at 28.2 billion yuan.

The company declared a final dividend of 0.2073 yuan per share, payable June 27 to shareholders on record as of June 19.

Shares jumped 3% during Friday's late-morning trading.

data.title: Hyundai Motor Set to Take Substantial Earnings Hit From Trump's Tariff — Market Talk
data.description: Hyundai Motor is set to take a substantial earnings hit from President Trump's 25% auto tariff and a likely 5% average-selling-price increase to ease the tariff impact for cars selling in the U.S., Nomura analyst Angela Hong writes in a note. Hong lowers her 2025 operating-profit forecast for the South Korean carmaker by 18%, noting Hyundai's 48% reliance on imports for vehicle sales in the U.S. She also revises down her 2025 EPS for Hyundai by 13%. Nomura cuts its target price for the stock by 11% to KRW250,000 but maintains a buy rating, noting that investors have priced in much of the tariff impact. Shares are 4.2% lower at KRW203,500.(kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)
